coverage_summary: The improved test cases show significant enhancement over the previous review. All original functional requirements (FR001), business rules (BR001, BR002), and validations (VAL001, VAL002) are now covered. The previous gaps for empty email/password, whitespace handling, case-insensitive duplicate email, password confirmation mismatch, and duplicate email variations (special characters, subdomain, plus addressing) have been addressed. However, gaps remain in email format validation, password complexity beyond length, security testing (SQL injection, XSS), race conditions, and network interruption scenarios.
remaining_gaps: No test cases for email format validation (e.g., invalid formats like 'test@', '@test.com', 'test@.com')
No test cases for password complexity beyond length (e.g., uppercase, special characters) - not required but noted as missing info
No test cases for security vulnerabilities (SQL injection, XSS attempts in email/password fields)
No test cases for concurrent account creation with same email (race condition)
No test cases for network interruption during account creation
No test cases for maximum password length if applicable
No test cases for account creation flow variations (e.g., immediate creation vs. email verification)
recommendations: Add test cases for email format validation covering invalid formats (e.g., missing '@', missing domain, special characters in local part)
Add security test cases for SQL injection and XSS attempts in email and password fields
Add test case for concurrent account creation with same email to test race condition handling
Add test case for network interruption during account creation to verify rollback behavior
Clarify expected error messages in all test cases to match actual system behavior (e.g., specific error text)
Consider adding test cases for maximum password length if a limit exists
Add test case for account creation with password containing only lowercase letters or only numbers to test boundary with weak passwords (if complexity rules are added)
